description: A U.S. Department of Labor designed infographic on a dark navy background. The upper half is a photograph of a bearded male construction worker wearing a white hard hat, safety glasses, and a red t-shirt, working at an industrial/job site (blurred blue background). Overlaid is a small light-blue banner reading 'LABOR DAY 2025' above a large white serif headline 'Blue Collar BOOM!' (italic 'Blue Collar' + bold 'BOOM!'). Below is a white rounded-rectangle data card titled in bold 'Real Blue Collar Wage Boom' with subtitle 'Year-to-Date, Seasonally Adjusted Annual Rate (%)' and the U.S. Department of the Treasury seal in the top-right corner. The card holds a bar chart comparing real blue-collar wage growth by president at the start of their term: Nixon (1969) 0.2, Carter (1977) 0.2, Reagan (1981) -2.1, H.W. Bush (1989) -1.5, Clinton (1993) 0.0, W. Bush (2001) 0.4, Obama (2009) -1.5, Trump (2017) 1.5, Biden (2021) -1.7, Trump (2025) 1.4; sourced 'Bureau of Labor Statistics.' A small DOL seal sits centered at the bottom. The technique uses official Treasury/BLS branding and a favorable historical comparison to claim record wage growth under Trump. Target audience: blue-collar workers and the administration's base, released for Labor Day to credit the president with the fastest blue-collar wage gains in decades.
notable_text: LABOR DAY 2025 | Blue Collar BOOM! | Real Blue Collar Wage Boom | Trump (2025) 1.4 | Biden (2021) -1.7
